# Navigating the technology conundrum: Balancing innovation and budget constraints

October 26, 2023 · 5 minute read

October 26, 2023 · 5 minute read

**Jump to:**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| icon-orange abcs | [How legal technology helps improve operations](#how-legal-technology-helps-improve-operations) |

![icon-orange abcs](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/05/abc_1-color.png)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| ``` icon-speaking bubbleicon-speaking bubble ``` | [Challenges to legal tech adoption](#challenges-to-legal-tech-adoption) |

![icon-speaking bubble](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/05/language_2-color.png)
![icon-speaking bubble](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%2025%2025%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | [Solving the legal technology conundrum](#solving-the-legal-technology-conundrum) |

![](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/07/tag_2-color.png)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%2025%2025%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

## 

Corporate law departments are in a conundrum when it comes to legal technology adoption. You see, while 72% of in-house legal department operations professionals see *using technology to simplify workflow* as a high priority, a mere 32% report an increase in *their legal technology budget*. 

What’s more, fully 90% say their department makes only slow to moderate progress in adopting new technology, according to the [2023 Legal Department Operations Index](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/en/insights/reports/legal-department-operations-index-eighth-edition-2023/form?gatedContent=%252Fcontent%252Fewp-marketing-websites%252Flegal%252Fgl%252Fen%252Finsights%252Freports%252Flegal-department-operations-index-eighth-edition-2023) from Thomson Reuters. The report also found that 78% of legal department operations professionals see cost control as a high priority.  

![](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/10/figure-1-bigger.jpg)![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20279%20472%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/10/figure-1-bigger.jpg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20279%20472%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

*Figure* *1**: The Technology Conundrum: Despite the desire to leverage new technology to enhance efficiency and effectiveness, slow progress and flat budgets remain significant barriers.* 

The Technology Conundrum is this: how do legal departments take advantage of the transformational power of modern legal tech without additional budget to do it? And how do they overcome slowness to adopt despite the huge gains in efficiency and effectiveness the whole team can make?  

## How legal technology helps improve operations

Despite the slowness to adopt, 53% of law departments reported that they increased their use of legal technology in the past year, mostly without increasing the share of their budget dedicated to legal tech. Instead, they may have seen faster adoption of the tools they already have. Their increased use of technology helped them increase their productivity and their attorneys’ output despite flat to declining budgets.  

Better technology tools help with efficiency and effectiveness in many ways. Here are two ways that align with the priorities of legal departments Thomson Reuters surveyed:  

***Controlling outside legal spend:*** The worked rate increases that law firms are requesting are “approaching historic highs,” according to the Legal Department Operations report. Still, clients are finding ways to mitigate the impact of these increases. One big strategy is shifting work from the Am Law 100 to midsize firms. This allows the client to realize a net cost savings. Legal departments that use a modern spend management system have visibility into rates across firms and regions to make this determination.  

**Simplifying work through technology:** Legal departments clearly recognize the value of e-billing and matter and spend management tools, with nearly 93% making some sort of investment in these tools. A modern matter management solution can save a legal department significant money over time. In fact, a Forrester [Total Economic Impact report on Thomson Reuters Legal Tracker](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/en/insights/reports/forrester-total-economic-impact-study/form) found that the system paid for itself in six months, largely due to savings in outside counsel rate increases.  

## Challenges to legal tech adoption

According to the Thomson Reuters report, law departments are making some progress with their technology adoption, but very few are making large scale advances each year. (See figure 2.) The report points to “inertial forces” to explain the pace.  

For instance, legal department leaders are very good at their legal work – and they are more comfortable sticking with what they know rather than risking breaking their system. And the large organizations these leaders support are often slow to change the way they work. Given how closely the law department works with other areas of the business, making large-scale change to law department systems can be a cross-functional challenge. 

![](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/10/Figure-2-bigger.jpg)![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20765%20321%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

![](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/sites/19/2023/10/Figure-2-bigger.jpg)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20765%20321%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)

*Figure* *2* *Most law departments are making modest advances in their technology adoption each year.* 

## Solving the legal technology conundrum

It is very clear from the report that legal operations professionals know how much technology can help their departments [manage spending](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/en/insights/articles/what-is-legal-spend-management) while delivering effective, efficient service. Many of them are finding support in the metrics they track. These are metrics that can help influence law department leaders when it comes to allocating the existing budget or even asking for additional resources. 

The report notes that it is common for law departments to track metrics related to cost efficiency. The opportunity for legal operations professionals is to add in metrics related to the law department’s effectiveness, how it enables the business, and how it provides protection. Some metrics to consider include rate of staff turnover, volume of work automated, uptake of existing tech stack, percentage of matters with desired outcomes, and department net promoter score by internal clients.  

With this data in hand, you can make a compelling case for technology that both saves money and improves effectiveness. Remember, you have three levels of stakeholders to convince: the executives who approve the legal department budget, the leaders of your department, and the individual users. By framing your argument in both efficiency and effectiveness, you’ll be able to motivate these three groups much more quickly.  

Solving the Technology Conundrum won’t be fast or simple – but it is possible. The [2023 Legal Department Operations Index](https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/en/insights/reports/legal-department-operations-index-eighth-edition-2023/form?gatedContent=%252Fcontent%252Fewp-marketing-websites%252Flegal%252Fgl%252Fen%252Finsights%252Freports%252Flegal-department-operations-index-eighth-edition-2023) can help you put the conundrum in perspective for your legal department. It provides insights and industry practices that can help your department make data-driven decisions and improve your strategic planning and technology adoption.

# How Legal Tech is Reducing Costs and Increasing Efficiency

Law firms' operational expenses typically account for between 45% to 65% of their revenue, making budget optimization essential for long-term success. Modern legal technology solutions offer practical methods to reduce these expenses while maintaining high-quality service. Legal professionals who embrace these solutions can optimize their budget and significantly lower their firm's costs through automated workflows, streamlined processes, and productivity-boosting tools.

## Automate Routine Tasks with AI and Machine Learning

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) technologies are transforming how law firms manage their routine tasks, leading to new opportunities for cost reduction and operational optimization. Recent surveys indicate that 75% of in-house lawyers are interested in using software more effectively for contract transformation.

### Document review and e-discovery

AI-powered document review systems have transformed traditional review methods. Legal teams spend an average of 4.5 hours each day manually reviewing contracts, but AI technology can reduce this time by 40%.

The system's benefits include:

Quick document analysis and processing

Lower human errors during reviews

Better accuracy when finding relevant details

Consistent document classification

### Contract analysis and management

AI-powered contract analysis tools have shown remarkable improvements in efficiency. Recent data indicates that 98% of users see time savings, and 90% see better accuracy and risk detection.

The comparison between traditional and AI-assisted contract reviews highlights these differences:

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/61ae3798a59b0b43a74ed448/668c188d-166b-48cf-b1a8-62a46bb8e8a0/Screen+Shot+2024-11-07+at+1.45.35+PM.png)

### Legal research assistance

AI-driven legal research tools have transformed how law firms conduct research and analysis. These systems utilize natural language processing to comprehend complex queries, instantly delivering relevant results. The technology can predict case outcomes, identify pertinent precedents, and provide evidence-based information that supports strategic decision-making.

By employing AI and machine learning solutions, law firms can significantly reduce overhead costs while enhancing the quality of service delivery. The trend toward legal AI solutions is growing, and many firms have reported substantial improvements in the efficiency and accuracy of their routine legal tasks.

## Streamline Billing and Invoicing Processes

Law firms now use modern legal billing systems that revolutionize their financial operations. Research shows that e-billing solutions help firms cut invoice processing costs by up to 20%. This technological change allows law firms to optimize their budgets and make their clients happier.

### E-billing systems

Modern electronic billing systems transform paper-based billing processes through automated validation and standardized formats. Companies that use e-billing systems see their billing errors drop dramatically. AI-powered invoice review automatically catches up to 90% of guideline violations.

Traditional billing systems are no match for electronic alternatives that deliver measurable benefits:

![](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/61ae3798a59b0b43a74ed448/f9630c79-e3c3-485b-b865-52c742592599/Screen+Shot+2024-11-07+at+1.59.49+PM+%281%29.png)

### Automated time tracking

Automated time tracking has transformed how legal professionals capture billable hours. Research shows that lawyers lose 9% of their billable hours because they lack the quickest way to track time. Modern time-tracking solutions give professionals these major benefits:

Up-to-the-minute capture of billable activities

Automatic entries from calendar events

Integration with practice management systems

Mobile access to log time remotely

### Improved reporting and analytics

Law firms can make informed decisions about their financial operations through advanced analytics capabilities. Integrated reporting systems help track performance indicators and highlight areas that need improvement. Research shows that law firms using automated reporting tools see a 15% improvement in collection rates and better understand:

Client payment patterns

Resource allocation efficiency

Profitability by practice area

Budget forecasting accuracy

These systems prove to be a strategic investment in improving firm efficiency, and most organizations achieve positive ROI within their first year of adoption.

## Conclusion

Law firms should assess their current technology stack against these proven money-saving solutions. The smart use of legal tech creates a competitive edge by lowering operational costs and improving service quality. Law firms that welcome these digital solutions set themselves up for steady growth while delivering excellent legal services to their clients. Research shows that investing in technology saves money immediately and benefits operations in the long run for progressive legal practices.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Budget limitations remain a primary barrier for legal tech adoption, as 66% of legal teams cite it as their main challenge, impacting resource allocation and technology investments.
Many legal departments lack dedicated IT resources, hindering their ability to implement and maintain new technologies effectively, as highlighted by the slow adoption rates reported.
Legal professionals often face tight schedules, making it difficult to allocate sufficient time for training on new technologies, which leads to underutilization of available tools.

enriched_points.point_title: Need for Ongoing Support and Resources
enriched_points.expanded_content: Continuous support is vital for successful legal tech adoption; firms require ongoing resources to ensure that technology remains effective and aligned with evolving legal practices.
